turns.name: JUSTICE BLACKMUN
turns.text: I think the time is passing, Mr. Chief Justice. We will let it go. Q I have one further question, if I may. Assuming j there is a three-day holding here. There was no cross-appeal.

turns.text: Might I read it just to give you a sense "Q° That’s up to the principal and not the student; is that it? A» That's right." These guidelines really are nothing muck more than guidelines. They are not well thought out procedures designed J to provide any degree of procedural protection for the student. Q I am sorry. I missed the earlier part. These guidelines are something that has developed since the decision in this case?

turns.name: ROOS
turns.text: They wefe developed a week before the Q Mr. Roos, I am not quite clear yet as to your suspension for one hour without any notice and for any reason is appropriate. Obviously then my next question, and I do not want to as it, will be two hours, three hours, until we get your point.

turns.name: ROOS
turns.text: Your Honor, it is generally our position that a severance from the school system, putting a child out of the school system as opposed to punishments that might happen end-of-the-day sort of thing, something that is as final and in the educational progress of the student and also has some There certainly is some area of line drawing, and I cannot deny that, Your Honor. But I think that the key is well-established statutory entitlement creates a property never considered historically or in the present such a well Honor; but, further than that, there is a constitutional There are several independent legislative requirements that the local community set up schools, that free public schooling be available for children between certain ages. It is a very pervasive scheme of entitlement. Q Mr. Roos, under your submission, is the age of the child or the grade of the pupil relevant in any situation? Or, putting it differently, as I recall, the ages of these school or, say, a sixth grader?

turns.name: ROOS
turns.text: In terms of the right to a prior hearing we do argue and argue forcefully that even a short-term I suspension can have stigmatizing consequences, can have educational consequences, and what not. Q Are there not a good many areas in which peremptory .reaction is allowed which involve stigmatizing and no prior hearing and no notice is given if there is probable cause?

turns.name: ROOS
turns.text: There may be some situations of that sort Your Honor, We would submit that there— mentioned arrest. There is no reason whatsoever for not holding a prior hearing in a school suspension case. In an arrest situation, where an emergency may occur, there is obviously need for quick action. Q Very often people are arrested in situations where there is no emergency. there is no reason advanced by our opponents and there is no reason that we can conjure up for not holding some form of suspension. So, it is our position that there is a protected interest. I do not think there is any question of an invasion thereof. It is our position that some form of prior hearing is appropriate. That is all, Your Honor.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: Very well. And I think your time is entirely consumed, Thank you, gentlemen. The case is submitted. submitted.] <
